subject: Address k8s plugin code smells reported by sonar
commit_message:
Address k8s plugin code smells reported by sonar

Make 'resources' argument to k8sclient.k8sclient.deploy an
optional kwarg, update its uses and document it.
Split off code reported complex from deploy into functions.
Tweak a nested if in tasks.

Signed-off-by: Miroslav Los <miroslav.los@pantheon.tech>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2006
Change-Id: I13a091de9207bab1c7d4eee3179263c5d994ffbf

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I downloaded the code, built the plugin, and installed it onto an instance of Cloudify Manager.   I attempted deploying a simple blueprint (that works with version 1.7.1 of the plugin) and encountered a failure during the install workflow.  Unfortunately the wrapper function hides the source of the error, but it happens in the create_and_start_container_for_platforms operation.

Patch Set 2:

The blueprint used was:
tosca_definitions_version: cloudify_dsl_1_3

description: >
  Simple blueprint to launch nginx as a "platform component"

imports:
  - http://www.getcloudify.org/spec/cloudify/4.5.5/types.yaml
  - types/k8splugin_types.yaml

inputs:

  replicas:
    description: Number of instances to launch
    type: integer
    default: 1

node_templates:
  web_server:
    type: dcae.nodes.ContainerizedPlatformComponent
    properties:
        name: 'nginx-plat'
        container_port: 80
        image: nginx
        docker_config:
            healthcheck:
                type: "http"
                endpoint: "/"
        application_config:
            web_root: "/web/root"
            log_level: "debug"
            workers: 46
        replicas: {get_input: replicas}

where types/k8splugin_types.yaml is an alias for the k8s-node-types.yaml file from the 1.7.2 code.

Patch Set 2:

As a point of comparison, the following blueprint (using a ContainerizedServiceComponent rather than a ContainerizedPlatformComponent) works correctly with 1.7.2

tosca_definitions_version: cloudify_dsl_1_3

description: >
  Simple blueprint to launch nginx as a "service component"

imports:
  - http://www.getcloudify.org/spec/cloudify/3.4/types.yaml
  - types/k8splugin_types.yaml

node_templates:
  web_server:
    type: dcae.nodes.ContainerizedServiceComponent
    properties:
        service_component_type: 'nginx-web'
        image: nginx
        docker_config:
            healthcheck:
                type: "http"
                endpoint: "/"
            policy:
                trigger_type: "docker"
                script_path: "/notify.sh"
    interfaces:
      cloudify.interfaces.lifecycle:
        start:
          inputs:
            ports:
              - '80:0'
            envs:
              MY_ENV1: value1
              MY_ENV0: value0

Patch Set 3:

Ok, I've mistakenly ommitted a check whether log_info and tls_info were present/nonempty in the first place. The filebeat and tls_config (from k8sconfig) are expected to be valid, though I added a check for those as well.

Sorry about the additional work. I have been working on setting up an ONAP cluster for testing this; it's been tricky with the resources I have there.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Ok, will redo the tls_info part soon.

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Please try now.

This patchset does fix the problem.   (Slightly different fix from what I tried independently, but equally effective.)   I will continue testing with more test blueprints.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

Built plugin from patchset 4, tested with a small assortment of test blueprints and also verified that the plugin could deploy the service components that we normally deploy at bootstrap time (snmptrap [soon to be removed from bootstrap], hv-ves, tca, prh, ves, holmes_rules, and holmes_engine.  

Found one problem in a test blueprint, but further testing shows that it predates these changes.  (See DCAEGEN2-2036.)  It's not a blocking issue, so no reason to hold back this review.
Change has been successfully merged by Jack Lucas
